MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John M. Marston of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Clayton Industries, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Lisha Wilson, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CLAYTON INDUSTRIES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, AN ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Clayton Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issue 2 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Wilson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and Peter Mason's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
